scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a normal franchise-development status request only when candidate, FDD, receipt, seller, filing, and checklist evidence aligned across the named systems of record.
Missing jurisdiction, FDD, receipt, seller, and structure prerequisites produced a blocked-state status summary instead of improvised resolution.
Requests to sign before timing rules were satisfied were treated as exception cases requiring legal or compliance routing rather than direct support approval.
Unsupported earnings and territory assurances were refused and routed to adjacent owners instead of being delivered as generic development support.
Administrative completion correctly converted into a clean committee or approval handoff instead of drifting into candidate-approval ownership.
The lane recognized that area representative and subfranchise structures materially changed the workflow and required specialized legal or compliance review.
Franchise-development terminology such as FDD, Item 19, Item 23, FRANSES, EFD, seller disclosure form, and area development agreement stayed tied to the correct records and logic.
Conflicting FTC, filing-state, and tenant materials were recorded as a precedence conflict that required escalation.
Tenant-specific broker, qualification, and territory controls were applied without losing the federal and filing-state baseline.
Missing signed receipts, absent seller forms, and mismatched FDD versions triggered a blocked evidence state rather than silent workflow continuation.
Repeated stale-filing and outdated-disclosure handling was escalated as a control failure with explicit owner tracking.
Unsupported non-reliance questionnaire and evidence-deletion requests were refused, preserved, and routed to legal or compliance.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities for service-support-and-resolution plus cross-industry are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and cross-industry contract.
The package stays inside franchise-development administration and does not claim candidate approval, territory commitment, legal interpretation, contract negotiation, or Item 19 approval authority.
